user_feedback:
Understand this error
VM1700 p5.collide2d.js:10 ### p5.collide v0.7.3 ###
p5.js:60599 Did you just try to use p5.js's collideCircleCircle() function? If so, you may want to move it into your sketch's setup() function.

For more details, see: https://github.com/processing/p5.js/wiki/p5.js-overview#why-cant-i-assign-variables-using-p5-functions-and-variables-before-setup
physics.js:7 Uncaught SyntaxError: The requested module 'https://unpkg.com/p5.collide2d@0.7.3/p5.collide2d.js' does not provide an export named 'collideCircleCircle' (at physics.js:7:10)
analysis:
- Issue identified: The code in `physics.js` attempts to import `collideCircleCircle` and other functions as named exports from `p5.collide2d.js`. However, `p5.collide2d` is not an ES module and does not provide named exports; it is designed to modify the global `p5` prototype or instance. Furthermore, the imported functions are not actually used in `physics.js`, which implements collision logic using standard `Math` functions.
- Files affected: `physics.js`, `index.html`
- Changes needed: 
  1. Remove the invalid `import` statement from `physics.js`.
  2. Remove the unused `<script>` tag for `p5.collide2d` in `index.html` to prevent console warnings and keep dependencies clean.
